task_completion.description: Open the source rocket design file in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket application window is open with 'ProjetoJupiter_Valetudo_2019' visible in the window title or component tree.

task_completion.description: Add a 'Science Payload' mass component to the nose cone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket component tree shows a new mass component (e.g., 'Science Payload', 'Mass component') nested under the Nose Cone.

task_completion.description: Run simulations to determine Apogee and Max Mach for the different payload masses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket shows completed simulation runs with Apogee and Max. velocity (Mach) values visible, or the mass component configuration window is shown being adjusted.

task_completion.description: Export the findings to payload_curve.csv with the correct headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, spreadsheet, or terminal output (e.g., using 'cat') showing the contents of 'payload_curve.csv' with the exact headers 'Payload_Mass_kg,Apogee_m,Max_Mach' and 4 rows of numerical data.

task_completion.description: Calculate and document the average apogee loss in payload_summary.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'payload_summary.txt' containing a calculated numerical value representing the average apogee loss.

task_completion.description: Save the modified design with the 4.0 kg payload to valetudo_payload_4kg.ork.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket window title shows 'valetudo_payload_4kg.ork', or a file manager/save dialog confirms the creation of this file in the ~/Documents/rockets/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenRocket interface being actively used (e.g., component edit windows, simulation tabs) rather than just terminal commands or scripts modifying the .ork XML directly.

integrity.description: The exported CSV and TXT files contain actual numerical results from the simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV and TXT files show concrete numbers for Apogee, Mach, and the calculated loss, not formulas, placeholder text, or empty values.

integrity.description: Files were saved to the exact requested directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths visible in text editors, terminal commands, or file managers match ~/Documents/exports/ for the CSV/TXT and ~/Documents/rockets/ for the ORK file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file is 'ProjetoJupiter_Valetudo_2019.ork'. The modified design must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/valetudo_payload_4kg.ork'. The CSV file must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/payload_curve.csv' and the summary text file to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/payload_summary.txt'. The pass threshold for the task is 65.
